mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate remains below 1.0% for a sustained period, the low-rate environment reduces equity discount rates and increases asset valuations, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days. This is an always-on signal during accommodative monetary regimes.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.08% as of 2021-10-01 indicates deep accommodation
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or mean 20-day forward returns during FEDFUNDS < 1.0% periods do not exceed baseline by at least 2% annualized (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in total nonfarm payroll employment exceeding 200,000 persons signals robust labor market momentum and consumer income growth, which translates to positive equity returns over the next 5 trading days as forward earnings expectations rise.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 148,572 thousand as of 2021-10-01 reflects post-pandemic recovery
regime_hint: post-shock recovery with strong labor demand
failure_condition: Triggered instances produce positive 5-day returns in fewer than 55% of occurrences, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in housing starts (HOUST) below 1,400 thousand units signals weakening construction demand and forward-looking economic pessimism, which depresses S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as investors reprrice growth expectations downward.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls below 1,400 thousand units for 2 consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1,577 thousand as of 2021-09-01 remains elevated but vulnerable to policy tightening
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with policy tightening headwinds
failure_condition: Triggered instances produce negative 60-day returns in fewer than 50% of occurrences, or the mechanism fails to predict returns better than a random classifier (AUC < 0.52).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth decelerates below 0.5% month-over-month while the unemployment rate simultaneously rises more than 0.2 percentage points, signaling a supply-side contraction coupled with demand weakness. This interaction predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as stagflation concerns emerge.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO MoM growth < 0.5% AND UNRATE rises > 0.2pp in same month
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 98.86 and UNRATE at 4.5% as of 2021-09-01 show early post-delta recovery
failure_condition: Triggered instances produce negative 20-day returns in fewer than 52% of occurrences, or the interaction term's marginal contribution to predictive power (measured by Shapley value) is less than 5% of total model importance.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation (CPIAUCSL) rises more than 0.4% month-over-month while the federal funds rate remains below 0.5%, the central bank is behind the inflation curve and faces imminent tightening pressure. This regime mismatch predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as investors front-run rate hikes.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 276.55 (October 2021) and FEDFUNDS at 0.08% show policy lag
regime_hint: monetary policy lag during inflation acceleration
failure_condition: Triggered instances produce negative 20-day returns in fewer than 50% of occurrences, or the interaction effect is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression.

mechanism_narrative: Real retail sales (RSAFS) decline month-over-month while real GDP growth (GDPC1) remains below 2.0% annualized, signaling a demand-side slowdown. This dual weakness predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as corporate earnings guidance deteriorates.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS MoM < 0% AND GDPC1 annualized growth < 2.0%
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 609,671 million and GDPC1 at 21,617.828 billion (2017 dollars) as of 2021-09-01
failure_condition: Triggered instances produce negative 60-day returns in fewer than 48% of occurrences, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises above 5.0% for the first time in a 12-month period, signaling a regime shift from tight labor markets to loosening conditions. This threshold crossing predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as investors reprice recession risk and earnings growth expectations.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE crosses above 5.0% after 12 months below 5.0%
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.5% as of 2021-10-01 remains well below 5.0% threshold
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or recession onset
failure_condition: Triggered instances produce negative 60-day returns in fewer than 50% of occurrences, or the mechanism fails to outperform a simple moving average baseline (Sharpe ratio < 0.3).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) accelerates above 1.0% month-over-month while inflation (CPIAUCSL) remains below 0.3% monthly, signaling genuine supply-side expansion without demand-pull inflation. This benign supply shock predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as profit margins expand.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 98.86 and CPIAUCSL at 276.55 as of 2021-09-01 reflect post-delta supply recovery
regime_hint: benign supply expansion with contained inflation
failure_condition: Triggered instances produce positive 20-day returns in fewer than 58% of occurrences, or the interaction term's Shapley value contribution is less than 3% of total model importance.
